title: Dark Dense Norwegian Sugar Crisp Flatbread
description: A fermented, glassy-jelly flatbread featuring dark soft brown sugar, perfect for a date night in Norway.
instructions: In a bowl, combine warm water, sugar ferment, and yeast. Let it sit for 10 minutes, until frothy.
Add butter, melted, then salt, and mix until combined.
Gradually add rice flour, mixing until a shaggy dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes, until smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover with plastic wrap, and let rise in a warm place for 1 hour.
Divide the dough into 4 equal pieces, shape into balls, and then flatten slightly into disks.
Place the flatbreads on the preheated teppan, and cook for 2-3 minutes on each side, until golden and crispy.
Transfer the flatbreads to a wire rack to cool.
Brush the flatbreads with melted butter, while still warm.
Serve immediately, garnished with a sprinkle of dark soft brown sugar, if desired.
Store leftover flatbreads in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 3 days.
